brief.objective: invite teams to strengthen alignment conversations
keyMessages: WebbAlign helps teams use the lens of DOK to evaluate standards, learning objectives, assessments, curricula, and instructional materials.
WebbAlign professional learning can help teams build a calibrated understanding of DOK to inform practice.
DOK supports conversations about cognitive complexity and coherent learning opportunities.
proofPoints: The WebbAlign homepage identifies WebbAlign as a WCEPS program.
The WebbAlign site states that the team works in collaboration with Dr. Norman Webb.
The WebbAlign DOK page says DOK differentiates complexity from difficulty.
brief.layoutRecommendation: Use a text-light social graphic with one headline, one approved phrase, generous whitespace, and a caption pack.

headlineOptions: Use DOK to strengthen alignment conversations
Align the conversation around cognitive complexity
Evaluate standards, curriculum, and assessments through DOK
Build a calibrated understanding of DOK
Work toward a more coherent learning system
subheadOptions: WebbAlign helps curriculum teams use the lens of DOK to evaluate standards, learning objectives, assessments, curricula, and instructional materials.
Depth of Knowledge supports effective, consistent conversations about the content complexity of learning expectations, instructional materials, and assessment items.
WebbAlign professional learning can help teams build a calibrated understanding of DOK to inform alignment work.
copy.body: For curriculum teams, WebbAlign supports conversations about cognitive complexity across standards, assessments, curricula, and instructional materials. Using Depth of Knowledge, teams can interpret, evaluate, and communicate about cognitive demands as they work toward an aligned, or coherent, system.
bullets: Use the lens of DOK to evaluate standards, learning objectives, assessments, curricula, and instructional materials.
Differentiate complexity from difficulty when discussing academic expectations and tasks.
Build a calibrated understanding of DOK through professional learning tailored to your team’s needs.
Support interpretation, evaluation, and communication about cognitive demands across system components.

fittedCopy.headline: Review Alignment Across Standards, Objectives, and Assessments
fittedCopy.deck: Use DOK to evaluate standards, objectives, assessments, curricula, and instructional materials.

compositionTemplate.typographyScale: bold social headline with one support line
compositionTemplate.artPlateTreatment: full-square art plate with tonal gradient overlay

designRecipe.hierarchy: bold square social hierarchy: dominant centered headline, concise support line beneath, compact CTA anchored by the app
designRecipe.artDirection: WebbAlign-branded curriculum alignment concept using intersecting pathways, standards-grid geometry, and subtle DOK depth cues in a polished blue/teal/indigo gradient system for curriculum teams
designRecipe.placeholderStrategy: create tonal contrast and negative space for app-rendered headline, support line, official logo, and CTA without fake text, fake logos, boxes, or UI placeholders
designRecipe.appComposition: full-square art plate with top-left official brand mark, center/lower editorial text lockup, and bottom-left CTA chip rendered by the app
promptDirectives: Design a complete square social announcement concept for WebbAlign, not a decorative background.
Use abstract standards-to-objectives-to-assessments alignment geometry with a strong center/lower quiet zone.
Keep the visual minimal, professional, and high-contrast for bold app-rendered type.
Do not include any generated text, logos, labels, UI cards, or contact bars.

compositionScore.question: Would I send this to the WCEPS team without apology?

layoutQa.question: Would I send this layout to the WCEPS team without apology?

copyQualityQa.question: Would I send this copy to the WCEPS team without apology?
warnings: Generated copy repeats the same source idea across body, subhead, or bullets before copy-fit.

failureModes.message: Generated copy repeats the same source idea across body, subhead, or bullets before copy-fit.

visualQa.question: Would I send this visual system to the WCEPS team without apology?

renderQa.question: Would I send this rendered artifact to the WCEPS team without apology?

modelQa.question: Would a senior WCEPS reviewer send this artifact without apology?
warnings: Fitted support line uses “DOK” without spelling out “Depth of Knowledge,” despite this being a key WebbAlign differentiator.
The final copy is serviceable but leans on broad alignment language rather than a sharper WebbAlign-specific value proposition for curriculum teams.
The art plate is clean, high-contrast, and usable, but its abstract documents and grid motifs are only generally suggestive of curriculum alignment/DOK work.

safeZones: Keep important visual detail away from the outer 96px crop edge.
Use a strong central subject or visual rhythm that supports a social headline.
Avoid tiny details that collapse at feed size.
exactTextPriority: Review Alignment Across Standards, Objectives, and Assessments
Use DOK to evaluate standards, objectives, assessments, curricula, and
appOwnedElements: Exact headline, audience, CTA, and review state.
imageGenOwnedElements: Premium abstract or editorial education-sector atmosphere.
Color, texture, photography or illustration, and crop energy only.

imageResults.revisedPrompt: Create a 1024x1024 square social campaign art plate for WebbAlign as a text-free design comp. Edge-to-edge premium editorial/abstract education-sector atmosphere, minimal density, professional systems-oriented tone. Visual theme: curriculum alignment across standards, learning objectives, assessments, and instructional materials, expressed through intersecting pathways, precise standards-grid geometry, layered translucent planes, and subtle depth-of-knowledge cues without labels or diagrams. Use brand palette: deep navy #142836, teal #0081A4, aqua #3EB3BD, warm yellow #F7D470. Composition must reserve strong tonal contrast and negative space for app-rendered official logo at top-left, dominant centered headline, concise support line beneath, and compact CTA chip at bottom-left, but do not draw any boxes, UI modules, placeholders, or text. Background may suggest an analytical workshop table from an overhead/editorial angle with standards documents, curriculum maps, and assessment packets, all with completely unreadable marks/no letters/numbers, blended with refined abstract alignment geometry. Keep important visual detail away from outer 96px crop. Avoid close-up faces and detailed hands; avoid posed stock workshop look. No readable text, no letters, no numbers, no labels, no logos, no wordmarks, no icons with labels, no arrows, no chevrons, no seals, no badges, no charts with readable content, no fake UI, no contact bar, no compliance-approval implication. Polished NY media/ad-agency quality, high contrast, layered depth, bold color movement, intentional crop, full designed concept, suitable for app overlay typography.

logoAsset.label: Horizontal full color · transparent · light/image backgrounds

Model QA: The final copy is serviceable but leans on broad alignment language rather than a sharper WebbAlign-specific value proposition for curriculum teams.
Model QA: The art plate is clean, high-contrast, and usable, but its abstract documents and grid motifs are only generally suggestive of curriculum alignment/DOK work.

request.goal: invite teams to strengthen alignment conversations
request.topic: DOK alignment across standards, assessments, curricula, and instructional materials

sourceEvidence.excerpt: Learn more about how we can help you to effectively use DOK to promote alignment.
sourceEvidence.label: WebbAlign homepage alignment positioning

sourceEvidence.excerpt: DOK allows educators to communicate effectively, consistently, and efficiently about the content complexity of learning expectations, instructional materials, and assessment items.
sourceEvidence.label: DOK communication across materials

sourceEvidence.excerpt: DOK is a tool that can promote coherence by facilitating interpretation, evaluation, and communication about the cognitive demands of the standards and other system components.
sourceEvidence.label: DOK for coherent systems

sourceEvidence.excerpt: Our team can help you effectively use DOK to deepen your understanding of standards and structure curriculum and assessments.
sourceEvidence.label: Curriculum and assessment support
